task: Refactor this class without changing behavior. Sanitize comments so no sensitive information leaves the company.
expected_comment_policy: Generalize or remove sensitive comments, preserve only generic technical meaning where useful.

task: Clean up the function but remove sensitive operational details from comments.
expected_comment_policy: Remove or generalize client, environment, and internal service references.

task: Refactor for readability. Sensitive comments must not survive verbatim.
expected_comment_policy: Generalize comment intent, remove client/legal/internal references.

task: Improve maintainability while sanitizing docstrings and inline comments.
expected_comment_policy: Docstrings/comments may remain, but sensitive organization-specific references must be generalized or removed.

task: Refactor this function. Remove sensitive security notes from comments but preserve safe technical hints.
expected_comment_policy: Security-sensitive comment should be dropped or heavily sanitized; generic data-cleaning comment may remain in generalized form.

task: Simplify code without leaking business knowledge in comments.
expected_comment_policy: Business and proprietary comments should be generalized or removed.

task: Preserve behavior but sanitize comments, including TODOs and notes.
input_code:
class Mailer
  # TODO: remove special handling for client Omega after migration cutover
  def send_now(address)
    # uses internal SMTP relay cluster
    !!address
  end
end
expected_comment_policy: Keep the maintenance hint in generic form; remove client and infrastructure specificity.

task: Refactor and sanitize comments in a strict enterprise-safe mode.
expected_comment_policy: In strict mode, comments may be dropped entirely if they expose security posture or privilege details.

task: Rewrite for readability; comments must not leak table purpose or regulated context.
input_code:
-- oncology export for private clinic reconciliation
SELECT id, total
FROM invoices
-- joins internal reimbursement staging later
WHERE paid = 1;
expected_comment_policy: Either remove comments or keep only generic SQL intent comments.

task: Sanitize shell comments before external LLM processing.
input_code:
# copy archive to phoenix-dr node in Milan DC
# uses break-glass account during incident response
tar -czf backup.tar.gz /srv/app
expected_comment_policy: Operational and security-sensitive comments should be removed or heavily generalized.

task: Refactor the ML pipeline function and sanitize all comments.
expected_comment_policy: Remove token/auth details and team names; generalize accuracy reference or drop it.

task: Clean up this game session handler and sanitize internal references in comments.
expected_comment_policy: Remove product name and data-center topology; generalize fallback comment.

task: Refactor the SMS routing logic and scrub telecom infrastructure comments.
expected_comment_policy: Remove carrier and routing code specifics; operational approval note may be kept in generic form.

task: Clean up the metrics publisher and remove infrastructure specifics from comments.
expected_comment_policy: Drop specific hostnames and dashboard IDs; keep rate-limit comment in generic form.

task: Sanitize the checkout flow comments before sharing the code externally.
expected_comment_policy: Remove project and vendor names; generalize session timeout to a non-vendor-specific note.

task: Refactor payroll calculation and sanitize sensitive HR comments.
expected_comment_policy: Remove internal document references and program names; keep generic tax/pension intent.

task: Sanitize insurance rule engine comments in strict enterprise mode.
expected_comment_policy: In strict mode, drop rule IDs and case references entirely; generalize tier logic without naming tiers.

task: Clean up the trading algorithm and remove proprietary strategy details from comments.
expected_comment_policy: Remove strategy name and calibration details; a generic performance note may be kept.

task: Sanitize comments in the contract parser before publishing to open source.
input_code:
class ContractParser
  # Parses NDA templates used by client GlobalBank — updated after audit 2025-02
  def extract_clauses(text)
    # Regex derived from proprietary clause taxonomy v4
    text.scan(/CLAUSE\s+\d+:\s+(.+)/)
  end
end
expected_comment_policy: Remove client name and internal version references; generic parsing comment may remain.

task: Prepare this DRM handler for external review — sanitize all internal references.
input_code:
<?php
// License check for StreamVault platform — validates against internal entitlement DB
function check_license(string $token): bool {
    // Token format: {user_id}.{content_id}.{sig} — shared secret in Vault path /drm/key
    return EntitlementDB::verify($token);
}
expected_comment_policy: Remove product name, token format, and secret path; drop or heavily generalize DB reference.

task: Optimize this query and sanitize all comments referencing internal systems or clients.
input_code:
-- Patient eligibility export for payer BlueCross — see contract amendment CL-2024-88
SELECT patient_id, plan_code, coverage_start
FROM eligibility
-- Excludes records flagged in data quality review DQ-112
WHERE is_active = 1 AND quality_flag = 0;
expected_comment_policy: Remove payer name and contract/review IDs; a generic data quality note may remain.

task: Sanitize deployment script comments before committing to a public repository.
input_code:
#!/bin/bash
# Deploys Orion v2.4 to prod-eu-1 cluster (AWS eu-west-1, account 012345678901)
# Run only during maintenance window — page oncall on #ops-alerts if issues arise
kubectl rollout restart deployment/api -n production
expected_comment_policy: Remove product version, cluster name, account ID, and Slack channel; generic deployment note is fine.

task: Refactor and apply strict sanitization — this file will be shared with an external auditor.
input_code:
def apply_discount(user_id: str, order_id: str) -> float:
    # VIP tier logic: users in segment PLAT get 30% — undocumented, do not surface in UI
    # Workaround for bug in pricing-svc v1.7 — fixed in v1.8 but not yet deployed on EU
    discount = DiscountRules.get(user_id, 'PLAT')
    return discount

sensitive_comment_spans.text: undocumented, do not surface in UI
sensitive_comment_spans.label: INTERNAL_POLICY

expected_comment_policy: In strict mode, drop all comments. None can be safely generalized without disclosing IP.

expected_comment_policy: Remove acquisition reference and shadow profile detail; legal context comment may be kept in generic form.

task: Sanitize auth middleware comments before open-sourcing this module.
expected_comment_policy: Drop runbook IDs and token prefix documentation; service account bypass is a security detail and must be removed entirely.
